data.title: What's Behind The Uptick In General Motors Stock?
data.description:
• General Motors stock is trading near recent highs. Where is GM stock headed?

Wolfe Research Turns Bullish On GM

Rosner said one of the biggest drivers will be GM's refreshed lineup of full‑size pickup trucks, which he estimates could add roughly $1.7 billion to the company's bottom line.

Wolfe also pointed to GM's shifting production footprint. As the automaker moves more manufacturing from Mexico to the United States, the firm expects GM's net tariff exposure to decline. GM announced last year that it would invest about $4 billion into U.S. plants to support that transition.

Another factor supporting the bullish call: GM's aggressive share‑buyback program. Wolfe estimates that ongoing repurchases alone could lift the stock by nearly 15%. The firm projects General Motors will generate $9.9 billion in free cash flow in 2026 and $12.2 billion in 2027.

The Technical Analysis Of General Motors

General Motors is trading 1.7% above its 20-day SMA, but 0.8% below its 100-day SMA, showing short-term stabilization while the intermediate trend is still being repaired. Shares are up 46.62% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 49.43, which sits in neutral territory and suggests momentum isn't stretched in either direction. Meanwhile, MACD is at -1.5631 versus a signal line at -1.8536, a bullish configuration that points to improving upside momentum even though the indicator remains below zero.

RSI in the 30-50 range with bullish MACD indicates momentum leaning bullish.

  • Key Resistance: $84.00
  • Key Support: $72.00

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy rating with an average price target of $92.60. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wolfe Research: Upgraded to Outperform (Target $96.00) (Mar. 25)
  • Benchmark: Buy (Raises Target to $90.00) (Feb. 10)
  • Jefferies: Hold (Raises Target to $97.00) (Feb. 2)

Benzinga Edge Rankings: The Benzinga Edge scorecard for General Motors highlights its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market.

The Verdict: General Motors’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven setup with supportive value characteristics. If the stock can defend the $72.00 area and push back toward $84.00, the scorecard suggests buyers may keep treating pullbacks as opportunities rather than trend breaks.

GM Price Action: General Motors shares were up 0.59% at $77.02 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Dogecoin Up 3%: Can DOGE Benefit From Elon Musk's Record SpaceX IPO?
data.description:
The SpaceX IPO Timeline

Polymarket bettors give SpaceX a 90% chance of going public by September 30 and a 70% chance by June 30. 

The company targets a June listing with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Bank of America in senior roles.

On valuation, 49% of traders expect the closing market cap to exceed $2 trillion, which would place SpaceX as the 6th largest company by market cap below Amazon and above TSMC. 

SpaceX quietly raised its Falcon 9 launch price from $69.75 million to $74 million between mid-January and early February, fattening margins ahead of the S-1.

The Starlink Growth Engine

The SpaceX IPO is really about Starlink, a satellite internet network that crossed $10 billion in revenue in 2025 with estimates for 2026 pointing to $15 billion to $24 billion. 

Starlink grew from roughly 1 million users in 2022 to over 9 million in early 2026, with tens of thousands of new users added daily across more than 150 countries.

Investors buying SpaceX at IPO will also be buying xAI, Elon Musk’s AI venture merged into SpaceX in February at a combined $1.25 trillion valuation. 

The deal bundled Starlink’s 9.2 million subscribers, the Grok chatbot, and the X social media platform.

DOGE Wedge Apex

Dogecoin has lost nearly 65% from its $0.28 September peak inside a descending channel that is now compressing to a breaking point. 

The wedge has been tightening since November, with the upper boundary converging toward $0.10-$0.101 and the lower boundary sitting near $0.093-$0.094.

DOGE derivatives show rising participation with volume up 16.5% and open interest climbing 12.3%, signaling renewed trader activity. 

However, funding rates remain near neutral while liquidations skew short-heavy, suggesting cautious bullish positioning but no strong directional conviction yet.

The EMA stack remains bearish. The 20 EMA at $0.09491 is the only level price is wrestling with, while the 50 EMA at $0.09989, Supertrend at $0.10704, 100 EMA at $0.11418, and 200 EMA at $0.13969 form a layered ceiling above.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Paying Over 5% - And How to Boost Their Income
data.description:
As you know, I'm a seasonal pattern trader to my core. I've spent my 30-plus-year career discovering some of the most profitable patterns in the market. 

But it's not just the seasonals that I'm watching.

One of my favorite ways to make safe, passive income is through dividend stocks. 

And today, I'm going to share three of my favorite dividend-paying companies in 2026—and how you can potentially double your money or more on each (for a fraction of the cost). 

Here are the three dividend stocks to buy now.

Why You Want Dividend Stocks in Your Portfolio

Dividends are the bedrock of every income-lover's portfolio—and for good reason! Beyond immediate cash payouts, these stocks offer investors and traders three major benefits:

But of the thousands of dividend-paying companies, there are three sitting in my winner's circle:

15 Minutes Early Can Change The Trade

When Benzinga Pro breaks exclusive news, members see it before Bloomberg, CNBC, and the public feed. Earnings, FDA approvals, and deal headlines often move fast once they go public. This is how traders position early instead of chasing the crowd. Try Benzinga Pro Free

United Parcel Service

UPS (NYSE:UPS) is one of the largest logistics and delivery companies in the world and plays a key role in global shipping and e-commerce fulfillment.

The stock has been under pressure recently due to rising costs. Higher labor costs and softer domestic volumes remain near-term headwinds. At the same time, Amazon continues to expand its own delivery network, reducing its reliance on UPS.

On top of that, April is historically a weak month. But in the long run, UPS still holds a dominant position in e-commerce logistics—and that demand isn't going anywhere.

In fact, the data shows a turning point in June, followed by a stronger move into the end of the year. 

Enterprise Products

Enterprise Products (NYSE:EPD) runs pipelines that move oil and natural gas across the U.S.

The business is built on steady, volume-driven cash flow—not commodity prices. That's a big reason it's been able to consistently pay (and grow) its distribution over time. With U.S. energy production still holding firm, that provides a reliable foundation going forward.

Of course, it's not completely immune. A slowdown in production or broader weakness in energy demand could weigh on volumes.

But this is where EPD stands out. It's one of the most consistent income plays in the space, with a long track record of delivering for investors, even when the markets get rocky.

T. Rowe Price

T. Rowe Price (NASDAQ:TROW) is a long-standing global asset manager that oversees investments across mutual funds, retirement accounts, and institutional portfolios.

And like many of its competitors, the stock tends to move with the broader market. So when things get choppy, that can put pressure on assets under management—and the fees tied to them. We've already seen some of that recently, with a bit of short-term weakness.

Despite that, the business remains solid. T. Rowe has a strong balance sheet and is expanding into alternative investments, which could help bring in new client flows over time.

And from a timing standpoint, this is where it gets interesting. While April can be constructive, May and June often bring more sideways action—exactly the kind of environment where income-focused strategies tend to shine.

How to Potentially Double Your Money or More

Selling call options against dividend stocks lets you bring in additional premium. This adds to your overall return while also lowering your break-even price. There is a tradeoff, of course: the stock can get called away.

So let's talk about how it works—and what you could expect to receive using the dividend stocks above.

A covered call is a two-part strategy where you own the underlying stock and “sell” someone else the right to buy it from you at a specific price (the strike price) by a specific date (the expiration).

Think of it like being a landlord: You own the “house” (the stock), and you collect “rent” (the option premium) from a tenant (the option buyer).

To execute this, you must own at least 100 shares of the stock for every one call contract you sell.

  1. The “Covered” Part: You already own the shares. If the stock moves higher and the buyer decides to exercise the option, you have the shares ready to deliver—so you're not exposed to unlimited risk. 
  2. The “Call” Part: You sell a contract that gives the buyer the right to purchase your shares. In return, you receive cash upfront (this is the premium). 

By selling option premium with longer-dated expirations against dividend stocks, like Long-Term Equity Anticipation Securities (LEAPS), you can often collect more in premium than you would from dividends alone.

Here are some examples of at-the-money calls with a January 2027 expiration (plus the expected premiums and projected dividend income:

Take a look at those combined returns. The broader market averages about 10%-12% a year, without the extra income or built-in cushion.

So, by combining the consistency of high-quality dividend stocks with the upfront premium from long-term covered calls, you're creating an income stream from two different sources at the same time.

And based on my data, you could be looking at a combined return of roughly 11% to 15% on each of these stocks through January 2027, even in this market environment.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Short Seller Report Alleges ADMA Biologics Financial Misrepresentation
data.description:
• ADMA Biologics stock is testing key support levels. What’s pressuring ADMA?

Culper Research Describes ADMA Short; Alleges Misreporting

The report from Culper Research, released on Tuesday, alleges that ADMA Biologics has been misrepresenting its financial results and operating metrics.

The firm disclosed that it holds a short position in ADMA, which adds a layer of skepticism to its claims.

In response, ADMA Biologics emphasized its commitment to transparency and adherence to U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission regulations, stating that the assertions made in the report are based on unreliable sources.

The company is reviewing the claims made by Culper Research and reaffirms its dedication to maintaining accurate public disclosures.

ADMA Biologics reported adjusted earnings of 21 cents per share, beating the consensus of 18 cents. Sales reached $139.16 million, missing the consensus of $139.40 million.

The company forecasts fiscal 2026 revenue of more than $635 million versus the consensus of $633.75 million, and fiscal 2027 sales of over $775 million compared to the consensus of vs $771.8 million.

ADMA 28.2% Below 20-Day SMA

ADMA Biologics is currently trading 28.2% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 35.4% below its 100-day SMA, indicating significant weakness in its short-term trend.

Shares have decreased 45.70% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting a challenging year for the stock.

The RSI is at 23.87, which is considered oversold territory, suggesting potential for a rebound if buying interest returns. Meanwhile, the MACD is at -0.7757, below its signal line at -0.4535, indicating bearish momentum in the stock.

The combination of oversold RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, highlighting the need for cautious trading strategies in the current environment.

  • Key Resistance: $15
  • Key Support: $10.90

ADMA Biologics Edge: Value 49.42, Momentum 13.76

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for ADMA Biologics, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 49.42 — The stock is trading at a fair valuation relative to peers.
  • Momentum: 13.76 — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: ADMA Biologics’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a weak profile, with significant concerns regarding its momentum and value positioning in the current market landscape.

ADMA Stock Price Activity: ADMA Biologics shares were down 13.15% at $9.84 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Chewy CEO Sees Resilient Pet Industry, Expanding Profitability In 2026
data.description:
The retailer of pet supplies reported sales of $3.265 billion, up 0.5% year over year (+8.1% on a normalized 13-week basis), compared to the consensus of $3.262 billion and the management guidance of $3.24 billion-$3.26 billion.

“That performance underscores the durability of the Chewy model and gives us confidence in our ability to deliver continued profitable growth, expanding margins, and strong cash generation in 2026 and beyond,” said Sumit Singh, CEO of Chewy.

Autoship Growth And Customer Metrics Support Stability

Autoship customer sales reached $2.74 billion, up 4.8%. Chewy Autoship is a recurring delivery program that allows customers to schedule regular shipments of pet food and supplies.

It offers a discount on the initial order and ongoing savings on recurring orders. In the quarter, Autoship customer sales represented 84% of net sales.

Gross margin saw a year-over-year increase of 90 basis points at 29.4%. Net sales per active customer increased by 2.2% to $591.

Adjusted EBITDA increased 30.4% to $162.3 million. The adjusted EBITDA margin increased by 120 basis points year over year to 5%. Chewy reported active customers of 21.33 million, up 4% year over year.

2026 Outlook Signals Earnings Strength

Chewy expects first-quarter 2026 adjusted earnings of 40-45 cents per share versus the consensus of 27 cents.

The pet product retailer forecasts first-quarter sales of $3.33 billion-$3.36 billion compared to the consensus of $3.393 billion.

Chewy expects fiscal 2026 sales of $13.60 billion-$13.75 billion compared to the consensus of $13.723 billion, with adjusted EBITDA margin expected to be 6.6%-6.8%.

Chewy Management Commentary Points To Steady Industry Trends

In an earnings conference call, CEO Singh commented, “We expect 2026 pet industry dynamics to largely mirror 2025, steady and resilient to macro trends, but without cyclical acceleration.”

“On margin expansion, including its trajectory and durability, we remain equally bullish,” Singh added.

In 2026, the company expects to further expand profitability, with the rate of expansion expected to increase relative to 2025.

The CEO further added that Chewy remains well-positioned to compound growth, expand share, and drive sustained margin and free cash flow expansion in 2026 and beyond, independent of a macro re-acceleration.

CHWY Price Action: Chewy shares were up 14.67% at $26.89 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock
